title: Sitting with the exhausted past version kindly
subtitle: A short visualisation for offering compassion to the version of you who lived through the difficult sleep years.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation practice from Day 16 of the SLEEP program. The scene: finding the version of you in the middle of the hardest sleep years and sitting with her with kindness.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Compassionate self-visualisation for processing the emotional residue of sleep deprivation.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 16 of the SLEEP program. Not a review of what went wrong, a visit to the person who was living through it.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take a slow breath in and let it go.

Picture the version of you during the hardest part of the difficult sleep years. She is lying in the dark. The clock says 3am, or 4am. Her body is tired but her mind is running. She is exhausted and frustrated and perhaps ashamed of how much sleep has come to matter.
You can see her from where you are now. Sit down beside her. You don't need to fix anything. You don't need to tell her it gets better. You're just there.
If there is something you want to say, say it quietly. Something simple. Something kind.
Stay with her for a minute. Then let the picture go. Open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. A visualisation for sitting with the hard feelings left over from the difficult sleep years.
